task: Create a Blue Life Commons and Ocean Intelligence visual asset foundation with vector logos, exact SVG infographics, Grok prompt metadata, and an initial generation experiment plan.
surface: Brand identity, README/deck assets, exact infographics, Grok-generated scene library, Ocean Intelligence handoff.
audience: Contributors, researchers, NGOs, educators, coastal communities, developers, and partner stakeholders who must understand and trust the ocean intelligence system quickly.
first_read: A source-led ocean commons turns reviewed artifacts into ethical guardians and public-good surfaces.

references.label: Blue Life Commons README
references.reason: Defines the three-layer model, audience, contribution workflow, and commons promise.

references.label: Blue Life Commons Catalog
references.reason: Confirms 58 current artifacts and the species/region/content spread.

references.label: Ocean Intelligence System README
references.reason: Defines guardians, connectors, dashboard surfaces, and the trust/continuity/reach boundary.

references.label: Ocean Intelligence Architecture
references.reason: Defines provenance, license, connector, signal, guardian, and dashboard invariants.

references.label: Generated Asset Quality Gate
references.reason: Defines the 30 point score and generated asset approval threshold.

keep: Clear distinction between exact SVG/code facts and generated emotional imagery.
Respectful animal distance and no unsafe wildlife interaction.
Teal, signal blue, and warm review accent from the existing README hero.
avoid: Generated text, labels, charts, maps, or dashboards.
Animal rescue or clinical imagery that implies decisions or exact live-animal locations.
Generic blue-tech gradients, whale-tail logos, anchors, globes, or node-cluster AI marks.
Conservation claims not sourced in repo artifacts.
direction.signature: The commons current: sourced artifacts moving through review into guardians and public surfaces.
system: Exact SVG infographics for labels and claims.
Grok scene generation in small batches with prompt IDs and metadata.
Generated images reviewed as draft source plates, not final facts.

artifacts.notes: Vector mark created as source-led artifact card plus living current; one-color companion added for small-size testing.
Monochrome SVG uses currentColor and avoids glow-dependent detail.
Horizontal lockup includes source, ethics, public artifact chips composed as exact SVG text.
Companion lockup for the private/runtime layer, aligned to guardian and connector language.
Exact SVG infographic; no generated text or claims.
Exact SVG triad diagram based on README and Ocean Intelligence README.
40 prompt metadata records prepared; first six generated exports have been inspected and scored.
Six Grok-generated JPGs inspected and scored. Three approved as draft source plates, two marked iterate, one marked restart.
Thirty Grok-generated JPGs across three batches summarized. Fifteen approved as draft source plates, fourteen marked iterate, one marked restart.
Approved as draft source plate at 27/30: strong reef scene, no text, no unsafe interaction.
Restart at 19/30: too much fake text, fake UI, and map-like detail.

checks.name: standards-loaded
checks.evidence: DESIGN_TASTE, DESIGN.md, AGENTIC_DESIGN_LOOP, ULTIMATE_AGENTIC_LOOPS, RED_TEAM_CHECKLIST, OUTCOMES, VISUAL_QA_GATE, generated asset gate, and repo ethics/source/style docs were read.

checks.name: repo-local-instructions
checks.evidence: blue-life-commons/AGENTS.md read before edits; Ocean Intelligence AGENTS.md read through GitHub.

checks.name: logo-vector-first
checks.evidence: Vector SVG master and one-color mark created before any rendered logo prompt.

checks.name: generated-media-inspection
checks.evidence: Grok batches 001-003 produced thirty JPGs and media-job.json files; contact sheets and QA summaries were created under _generated and docs/visual-system.

checks.name: animal-safety
checks.evidence: Prompt constraints explicitly ban unsafe proximity, feeding, touching, baiting, crowding, captivity glamour, exact sensitive locations, and anthropomorphic claims.

score.notes: Planning, vector identity, exact infographic foundation, and Grok batch execution are strong. Score remains iterate because many paper/screen/human workflow scenes need crop, edit, or regeneration before publication.

known_risks: Grok saved 30 local files successfully, but paper/screen scenes can still produce pseudo-text and fake UI.
Generated animal scenes may need expert/editor review before educational use.
The private ocean-intelligence-system repo is not checked out locally, so companion assets are prepared here as a handoff rather than written there.
